text: Early sport specialization (at age 12 or younger) does not increase the risk of injury or illness in youth elite athletes entering specialized training programs.
Being a single-sport athlete in the two years prior to entering a specialized sport academy does not increase injury or illness risk compared to multi-sport involvement.
Higher performance level at enrollment does not predict greater injury or illness risk during a 26-week sport academy program.
Among youth elite athletes in a specialized sport academy, 39% reported early specialization (at age 12 or younger).
Neither early single-sport specialization nor current performance level are risk factors for injury or illness in youth elite athletes who have already been selected into talent programs.
